translation: Glorify the name of your Lord, the Most High,
Who created and gave the things created good shape;
And Who determined [the span of existence for the things created] and guided them,
And Who brought out the verdure [on the earth],
And then made it into dark rubble,
We shall make you recite and then you will not forget
notes: It is apparent from the context that the Prophet (peace on him) was, in the initial period of his prophethood, anxious that he might not forget the Verses that were being revealed to him. He was reassured as in this and the next two Verses.

arabic: إِلَّا مَا شَآءَ ٱللَّهُۚ إِنَّهُۥ يَعۡلَمُ ٱلۡجَهۡرَ وَمَا يَخۡفَىٰ
transliteration: Illa ma shaa Allahu innahu yaAAlamu aljahra wama yakhfa
translation: Except for what Allah wills. HE knows what is manifest and what is hidden.
And We shall make things easy for you.
Should the divine Words of the Qur’aan benefit them, do recite these to them!
He who fears (Allah) shall take heed there from,
And he who is wicked will shun it;
He who will burn in the Great Fire.

arabic: ثُمَّ لَا يَمُوتُ فِيهَا وَلَا يَحۡيَىٰ
transliteration: Thumma la yamootu feeha wala yahya
translation: Then he will neither die in it, nor live.
He will surely attain salvation who keeps himself pure [in worldly life],
And oft-remembers his Lord´s name and then prays to Him.
But no, you prefer this worldly life!
Whereas the Hereafter is better and enduring.
This fact, indeed, was [also stated] in the earlier Scriptures –
The Scriptures of Abraham and Moses.
